message:
fix: changelog 'since' label uses input string; empty validation vec; restore error panel border

src/cli/commands/changelog.rs::resolve_since
  When `--since-tag <T>`, `--since-commit <C>`, or `--since <S>` was
  passed, the second tuple element ("the human label of the since
  cutoff" used in changelog headers and JSON output) was being set
  to the resolved RFC3339 timestamp string. That meant
      br changelog --since-tag v0.1.43
  produced a header like "Since 2026-04-15T19:42:11+00:00" instead
  of "Since v0.1.43" — strictly less informative. Now:
    - --since-tag <T>     → label = T
    - --since-commit <C>  → label = first 7 chars of C (or all of C
                            if shorter)
    - --since <S>         → label = S verbatim (works for both the
                            `parse_relative_time` arm and the
                            `parse_flexible_timestamp` arm)
  In-file unit test that asserted the formatted-RFC3339 form is
  updated to the canonical-Z form
  (`"2023-01-01T00:00:00Z"`) — the only timestamp-formatted assertion
  left in this file is for the no-flag path, which still produces a
  derived label.

src/error/mod.rs::BeadsError::from_validation_errors
  The function dispatched on `errors.len() == 1` (single → flat
  Validation variant) vs. else (many → ValidationErrors variant).
  An empty input would previously hit the "single" arm, index `[0]`,
  and panic before the dispatch could even decide. Add an explicit
  `errors.is_empty()` arm that constructs `Self::ValidationErrors {
  errors }` (i.e. an empty wrapper, no panic). Callers that pass an
  empty Vec are arguably wrong, but a panic deep inside the error
  conversion is the wrong way to tell them so.

src/output/context.rs (error panel rendering)
  The error-panel render path had its `.border_style(...)` line
  commented out, leaving error panels rendered with the default
  border colour. Re-enable it as
      .border_style(self.theme().error.clone())
  so error panels visually distinguish themselves again. Switched
  from the direct `.theme.` access (no longer valid after
  `OutputContext` started gating its theme through a `theme()`
  accessor) to the accessor form.
